teaching_arc.entry_point: Speedometer hook, average speed over an interval vs speed 'right now'.
teaching_arc.progression: Average ROC (secant) → limit as h→0 → visual_lab → theorem linking f′(a) → velocity & marginal cost → corner DNE.
teaching_arc.destination: Students distinguish average vs instantaneous rates and know when the limit of difference quotients fails.

topic_goal: Build instantaneous rate of change as the limit of secant slopes, bridge from Session 2 limits to Session 3 derivatives.
topic_takeaways: Average ROC = secant slope; instantaneous ROC = limit as h→0
When the limit exists it equals f′(a) and tangent slope
Apply to velocity and marginal cost via difference quotients
Average over a finite interval ≠ instantaneous on nonlinear curves
Corners: unequal one-sided difference-quotient limits ⇒ no instantaneous rate
style_notes: Welcome back: Session 2 complete through IVT (Topic 7); Session 3 opens derivatives.
Callback to Session 2 limits and 0/0 algebra when canceling h.
Narrate secant_tangent scriptedTimeline: call h, then let timeline shrink it toward 0.
visual_lab s04: demo one tab (~30s) on YouTube; mention site tabs.
math_solution_steps: target step_1…step_n in reveal order for generation.
pause_and_reveal on s08 and s12, real think beats, not decorative.
Do not re-introduce Arian from scratch.
transition_style: Hook with motion → formal limit → lab → worked examples → misconception → corner case → tease Topic 9.

slides.narration_seed: Welcome back, Session 2 limits are done. Topic 8 opens Session 3: rates at a single instant, not over an interval.
slides.transition_from_previous: Bridge from Session 2 IVT into derivative intuition.
slides.transition_to_next: Roadmap on learning objectives, path_topic_08 current.

slides.narration_seed: Orient on roadmap: Topics 1–7 done. Today instantaneous rate; next Topic 9 formalizes f′(a).
Speedometer reads now; trip average is secant slope. Timeline shrinks h, secant approaches tangent.
Average ROC = Δy/Δx = secant slope. Set x₂=a+h, this is the difference quotient we will limit.
Instantaneous ROC = limit of secant slopes as h→0. Session 2 limit machinery applies.
Lab: three h presets. Demo tab B, secant rotates to tangent. Site: try negative h and all tabs.
h-form and two-point form are equivalent. Negative h still approaches the same tangent.
theorem_box: when differentiable, instantaneous ROC = f′(a) = tangent slope. Preview Topic 9.
3x² at x=2: step_1 definition, step_2 expand, step_3 collect, step_4 cancel h, step_5 answer 12.
Pause: find v(1) for s(t)=t³−6t²+9t. Use h-formula before reveal.
Reveal: expand, linear h cancels, limit 0 m/s at t=1. Graph shows flat tangent.
misconception_compare: average over [3,4] is 8000, not instantaneous at t=3 (~5545).
Correct limit: 8000 ln 2 ≈ 5545. step_1 quotient, step_2 factor, step_3 ln 2 limit, step_4 ≈5545.
Pause: |x−2| at x=2. One-sided difference quotients: left −1, right +1.
step_1 right limit 1, step_2 left limit −1, step_3 DNE. Callback Topic 5 one-sided limits.
Marginal cost = C′(50). Cancel h → $30/unit. Economics application.
Three formulas: average, h-form, two-point. Secant→tangent widget recap.
Recap five bullets. Tease Topic 9: formal f′(a) and tangent line equation.
slides.transition_to_next: Hand off to Topic 9 formal derivative definition.

slides.narration_seed: Optional: x sin(1/x), difference quotient oscillates, f′(0) DNE. Contrast x² sin(1/x) in Topic 9.
